Name: Build a large language model (from scratch)
Description: Code-first learning path aligned with the LLMs-from-scratch book, focused on implementing core LLM components in PyTorch.
Topics: Foundations and Lifecycle; Data; Model architecture; Post training; Deployment and serving; Cost and performance
Skills: Tokenization and text preprocessing; Attention mechanism implementation; GPT architecture coding; Pretraining pipelines; Classification fine-tuning; Instruction fine-tuning; PyTorch fundamentals; Training loop optimization; LoRA adaptation
Roles: ML engineer; LLM application engineer; Platform engineer; Evaluation engineer
Tags: LLMOps; PyTorch; LLM architecture; Attention; GPT; Pretraining; Fine-tuning; LoRA
Lessons: Ch 1: Understanding large language models; Ch 2: Working with text data; Ch 3: Coding attention mechanisms; Ch 4: Implementing a GPT model from scratch; Ch 5: Pretraining on unlabeled data; Ch 6: Finetuning for text classification; Ch 7: Finetuning to follow instructions; Appendix a: Introduction to PyTorch; Appendix d: Adding bells and whistles to the training loop; Appendix e: Parameter-efficient finetuning with LoRA
Goals: Understand LLM internals by implementing models directly in code; Develop practical PyTorch skills for training and adaptation workflows; Build a strong foundation for evaluating and extending modern LLM systems
